channel.title: Gitea Limited — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Gitea Limited (gitea).

item.title: CVE-2026-58426 · 9.6 CRITICAL — Gitea Actions Artifacts V4 signed URL HMAC ambiguity allows cross-repository artifact read and cross-task upload-state write
item.description: Gitea Actions Artifacts V4 signed URL HMAC ambiguity allows cross-repository artifact read and cross-task upload-state write

item.title: CVE-2026-58424 · 8.9 HIGH — Permanent Fork PR Workflow Approval Gate Bypass
item.description: Permanent Fork PR Workflow Approval Gate Bypass

item.title: CVE-2026-58423 · 7.7 HIGH — LFS authentication bypass via malformed SSH sub-verb allows unauthorized read access to private repositories
item.description: LFS authentication bypass via malformed SSH sub-verb allows unauthorized read access to private repositories

item.title: CVE-2026-58422 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Improper authorization on OAuth sign-in callback silently re-enables administrator-disabled accounts
item.description: Improper authorization on OAuth sign-in callback silently re-enables administrator-disabled accounts

item.title: CVE-2026-58421 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Unauthenticated ReDoS via CODEOWNERS pattern matching allows denial of service
item.description: Unauthenticated ReDoS via CODEOWNERS pattern matching allows denial of service

item.title: CVE-2026-58419 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Notification API leaks private issue metadata after access revocation
item.description: Notification API leaks private issue metadata after access revocation

item.title: CVE-2026-58418 · 6.5 MEDIUM — SSRF via HTTP Redirect in Repository Migration
item.description: SSRF via HTTP Redirect in Repository Migration

item.title: CVE-2026-28744 · 8.1 HIGH — Gitea Git smart HTTP bypasses repository token scopes for bearer tokens
item.description: Gitea versions up to and including 1.26.1 allow Git smart HTTP requests authenticated with bearer tokens to bypass repository token scope checks.

item.title: CVE-2026-28740 · 7.1 HIGH — Gitea LFS object reuse bypasses Code-unit authorization
item.description: Gitea versions up to and including 1.26.2 allow Git LFS object reuse to authorize private source objects for users who have repository access but lack Code-unit access.

item.title: CVE-2026-28737 · 8.7 HIGH — Gitea 3D file viewer allows stored XSS through glTF extensionsRequired
item.description: Gitea versions from 1.25.0 before 1.26.0 allow stored cross-site scripting through the extensionsRequired field in glTF files rendered by the 3D file viewer.

item.title: CVE-2026-28705 — Gitea repository dumps write release assets using unsafe path names
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.25.5 use release tag names and asset names as filesystem path components when dumping release assets, allowing specially crafted names to affect dump output paths.

item.title: CVE-2026-28699 · 8.1 HIGH — Gitea Basic Auth bypasses OAuth2 access token scopes
item.description: Gitea versions up to and including 1.26.1 allow OAuth2 access token scope enforcement to be bypassed through HTTP Basic authentication.

item.title: CVE-2026-27783 · 4.3 MEDIUM — Gitea issue-template APIs bypass repository unit authorization
item.description: Gitea versions up to and including 1.26.1 do not enforce repository-unit authorization on issue-template API endpoints.

item.title: CVE-2026-27780 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Gitea pre-receive hook can miss branch-protection checks after scanner errors
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.26.0 do not fail closed on bufio.Scanner errors while processing pre-receive hook input, allowing oversized input to bypass branch-protection checks.

item.title: CVE-2026-27779 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Gitea forwarded-proto handling allows public URL spoofing
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.25.5 accept malformed or injected forwarded-proto values when detecting public URLs, allowing spoofed canonical URL generation.

item.title: CVE-2026-27775 · 8.8 HIGH — Gitea pre-receive hook permission cache allows full repository write access
item.description: Gitea 1.25.5 caches a branch-specific write-permission result across multiple refs in one pre-receive hook session, allowing a per-branch maintainer-edit grant to be reused for other refs and escalate to full repository write access.

item.title: CVE-2026-27771 · 8.2 HIGH 🌩️ — Gitea Composer package source links use insufficient permission checks
item.description: Gitea versions up to and including 1.26.1 have insufficient permission checks for Composer package source links, which can expose private or internal package source information.

item.title: CVE-2026-27761 · 4.3 MEDIUM — Gitea repository feeds bypass API token scope enforcement
item.description: Gitea versions up to and including 1.26.2 allow repository RSS and Atom feed endpoints to bypass API access token scope checks, exposing private repository commit data to tokens without the required repository scope.

item.title: CVE-2026-27660 — Gitea draft releases use insufficient permission checks
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.25.5 allow draft release data or attachments to be accessed without the required write permission.

item.title: CVE-2026-27657 — Gitea email settings allow changing another user's primary email address
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.25.5 allow a user to change another user's primary email address.

item.title: CVE-2026-26307 — Gitea git grep search lacks a timeout
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.25.5 do not enforce a timeout on git grep searches, allowing expensive searches to consume server resources.

item.title: CVE-2026-26292 — Gitea LFS mirror synchronization bypasses migration HTTP transport restrictions
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.25.5 do not use the migration HTTP transport for LFS push and sync mirror operations, bypassing the configured migration transport protections for those LFS requests.

item.title: CVE-2026-26247 — Gitea OAuth2 PKCE S256 challenges are not enforced during token exchange
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.25.5 do not persist the OAuth2 PKCE S256 challenge method correctly during authorization, allowing token exchange without the expected verifier check.

item.title: CVE-2026-26232 — Gitea OAuth2 authorization codes lack expiry and reuse enforcement
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.25.5 do not consistently enforce OAuth2 authorization code expiry and single-use behavior during token exchange.

item.title: CVE-2026-26231 · 8.5 HIGH — Gitea maintainer-edit permissions allow unauthorized commits to readable repositories
item.description: Gitea versions up to and including 1.26.1 allow the Allow edits from maintainers permission path to authorize commits to repositories that the user can read but should not be able to write.

item.title: CVE-2026-25782 — Gitea tracked-time deletion can target entries from another issue
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.25.5 look up tracked-time entries by time ID without scoping the lookup to the issue in the request URL, allowing deletion attempts to target entries from another issue.

item.title: CVE-2026-25779 — Gitea redirect handling permits open redirects through backslash paths
item.description: Gitea versions up to and including 1.25.4 allow redirect bypasses through raw or percent-encoded backslashes in redirect_to values.

item.title: CVE-2026-25718 — Gitea template repository generation mishandles symlinked paths
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.25.5 mishandle path resolution during template repository generation, allowing template processing to read or write through symlinked or otherwise non-regular paths.

item.title: CVE-2026-25714 · 4.3 MEDIUM — Gitea user organization API bypasses public-only token filtering
item.description: Gitea versions up to and including 1.26.1 do not apply public-only token filtering consistently to the user organization API, leaving an incomplete fix for CVE-2025-68941.

item.title: CVE-2026-25712 — Gitea organization permission APIs expose private visibility information
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.25.5 have insufficient visibility checks in organization permission APIs for hidden members and private organizations.

item.title: CVE-2026-25038 — Gitea private organization labels are visible to unauthorized users
item.description: Gitea 1.26.2 allows unauthorized users to access labels of private organizations.

item.title: CVE-2026-24690 — Gitea pull-request branch updates use insufficient permission checks
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.25.5 have insufficient permission checks for updating or rebasing pull request branches.

item.title: CVE-2026-24451 — Gitea fork synchronization can expose private parent repository data
item.description: Gitea 1.26.2 allows fork synchronization to continue after a parent repository changes from public to private, exposing data to a fork that should no longer be authorized.

item.title: CVE-2026-22874 · 9.6 CRITICAL — Gitea webhook and migration allow-list filtering permits SSRF
item.description: Gitea versions up to and including 1.26.2 have incomplete SSRF protection in webhook and migration allow-list filtering.

item.title: CVE-2026-22555 · 8.1 HIGH — Gitea organization forks can expose organization secrets without create permission
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.26.0 allow API users to fork a repository into an organization without first passing the CanCreateOrgRepo check, which can expose organization secrets.

item.title: CVE-2026-22547 — Gitea repository creation accepts invalid field values
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.25.5 lack validation constraints for repository creation fields, including length-limited template fields and trust model or object format values.

item.title: CVE-2026-20909 — Gitea tracked-time list endpoint has insufficient permission checks
item.description: Gitea versions before 1.25.5 have insufficient permission checks when listing tracked time entries.

item.title: CVE-2026-20896 · 9.8 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Gitea Docker image trusts spoofable reverse-proxy headers by default
item.description: Gitea Docker image versions up to and including 1.26.2 use REVERSE_PROXY_TRUSTED_PROXIES=* by default, allowing any source IP to impersonate a user when reverse-proxy authentication headers such as X-WEBAUTH-USER are enabled.

item.title: CVE-2026-20779 · 7.1 HIGH 🌩️ — Gitea TOTP single-use enforcement defect allows OTP replay
item.description: Gitea versions from 1.5.0 before 1.26.3 have a TOTP single-use enforcement defect that allows a valid TOTP code to be accepted more than once across web two-factor authentication flows and the Basic Auth X-Gitea-OTP path.

item.title: CVE-2026-20706 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Gitea repository archive downloads bypass token scope checks
item.description: Gitea versions up to and including 1.26.1 allow repository archive downloads to bypass token scope checks on the web archive download endpoint.

item.title: CVE-2026-20912 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Gitea: Cross-Repository Authorization Bypass via Release Attachment Linking Leads to Private Attachment Disclosure
item.description: Gitea does not properly validate repository ownership when linking attachments to releases. An attachment uploaded to a private repository could potentially be linked to a release in a different public repository, making it accessible to unauthorized…

item.title: CVE-2026-20904 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Gitea: Broken access control in OpenID visibility toggle enables cross-user visibility changes
item.description: Gitea does not properly validate ownership when toggling OpenID URI visibility. An authenticated user may be able to change the visibility settings of other users' OpenID identities.

item.title: CVE-2026-20897 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Gitea Git LFS Lock Deletion Broken Access Control (Cross-Repo IDOR)
item.description: Gitea does not properly validate repository ownership when deleting Git LFS locks. A user with write access to one repository may be able to delete LFS locks belonging to other repositories.

item.title: CVE-2026-20888 · 4.3 MEDIUM — Gitea Pull Requests Auto-Merge: Read-Only Users Can Cancel Scheduled Auto-Merge via Web Endpoint (Authorization Bypass)
item.description: Gitea does not properly verify authorization when canceling scheduled auto-merges via the web interface. A user with read access to pull requests may be able to cancel auto-merges scheduled by other users.

item.title: CVE-2026-20883 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Gitea Stopwatch API Missing Authorization Check Leads to Post-Revocation Information Disclosure
item.description: Gitea's stopwatch API does not re-validate repository access permissions. After a user's access to a private repository is revoked, they may still view issue titles and repository names through previously started stopwatches.

item.title: CVE-2026-20800 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Notification API Leaks Private Repository Issue Titles After Collaborator Permission Revocation
item.description: Gitea's notification API does not re-validate repository access permissions when returning notification details. After a user's access to a private repository is revoked, they may still view issue and pull request titles through previously received n…

item.title: CVE-2026-20750 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Gitea Organization Projects Cross-Organization Authorization Bypass via Project ID (IDOR)
item.description: Gitea does not properly validate project ownership in organization project operations. A user with project write access in one organization may be able to modify projects belonging to a different organization.

item.title: CVE-2026-20736 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Gitea Web Attachment Deletion: Cross-Repository Unauthorized Deletion via Missing Repo Ownership Check
item.description: Gitea does not properly verify repository context when deleting attachments. A user who previously uploaded an attachment to a repository may be able to delete it after losing access to that repository by making the request through a different reposi…

item.title: CVE-2026-0798 · 3.5 LOW — Gitea Release Email Notifications Leak Private Repository Release Details After Access Revocation
item.description: Gitea may send release notification emails for private repositories to users whose access has been revoked. When a repository is changed from public to private, users who previously watched the repository may continue to receive release notifications…

item.title: CVE-2024-6886 · 10 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Inproper Sanitation of field leading to stored XSS
item.description: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (XSS or 'Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in Gitea Gitea Open Source Git Server allows Stored XSS.This issue affects Gitea Open Source Git Server: 1.22.0.
